get.description:
Get Properties

GET /config/object/properties/

Gets the configuration properties for the specified category. If the category is not provided, then properties of all categories will be retrieved.

Properties are:
user-scope: defines whether user accounts should be treated as GLOBAL or NAMESPACE.

  • In GLOBAL scope, users are global and are can be shared across namespaces. In this case, the default namespace associated with a user determines the namespace for object operations and there is no need to supply a namespace for an operation.
  • If the user scope is NAMESPACE, a user is associated with a namespace, so there might be more than user with the same name, each associated with a different namespace. In NAMESPACE mode a namespace must be provided for every operation. Must be set before the first user is created.

The default setting is GLOBAL.

Query Parameters

category - Category: Optional. The category for which to retrieve configuration properties. If this is not provided then defaults to "ALL"

Required Roles

Requires one of the following roles:

  • SYSTEM_ADMIN
  • SYSTEM_MONITOR

Response Body

Configuration properties for the specified category or "ALL"

Field Description Type Notes
property_info
properties Configuration properties for the given category
properties Map

put.description:
Set Properties

PUT /config/object/properties/

Sets the configuration properties for the system.

Properties are:
user-scope: defines whether user accounts should be treated as GLOBAL or NAMESPACE.

  • In GLOBAL scope, users are global and are can be shared across namespaces. In this case, the default namespace associated with a user determines the namespace for object operations and there is no need to supply a namespace for an operation.
  • If the user scope is NAMESPACE, a user is associated with a namespace, so there might be more than user with the same name, each associated with a different namespace. In NAMESPACE mode a namespace must be provided for every operation. Must be set before the first user is created.

The default setting is GLOBAL.

Required Roles

Requires one of the following roles:

  • SYSTEM_ADMIN

Prerequisites

The following conditions must be met in order to call this operation.

  • Cluster state should be STABLE
  • "user_scope" always exists and is set to default value

Request Payload

<property_update>
  <properties>
    <properties></properties>
  </properties>
</property_update>

Response Body

Response indicating Success or Failure to update configuration properties

post.description:
Add Smtp Server

POST /vdc/smtp/config/server

Adds SMTP server. If port is not provided default port for a particular encryption is automatically picked. API adds SMTP server successfully once it has internally established a successful connectivity to the SMTP server being configured. If SMTP server is not configured to support encryption or port provided API will fail to add SMTP server successfully.

Required Roles

Requires one of the following roles:

  • SYSTEM_ADMIN

Request Payload

<smtp_server>
  <server></server>
  <port></port>
  <certificate></certificate>
  <security></security>
  <authenticationEnabled></authenticationEnabled>
  <user_security_model>
    <username></username>
    <password></password>
  </user_security_model>
</smtp_server>

Response Body

Response indicating Success or Failure to configure SMTP server

bucketName Bucket name

Query Parameters

start_time Starting time for the sample(s) in ISO-8601 minute format. Time must be a multiple of 5 minutes or an error will be returned.
end_time Ending time for the sample(s) in ISO-8601 minute format. Time must be a multiple of 5 minutes or an error will be returned.
sizeunit Unit to be used for calculating the size on disk (KB, MB, GB, KiB, MiB, GiB and BYTE. GB is default value). For legacy compatibility, KB, MB, and GB Units are considered equivalent to KiB, MiB, and GiB respectively. If needed, use BYTE Unit to convert to KB, MB and GB.

Required Roles

Requires one of the following roles:

  • SYSTEM_ADMIN
  • SYSTEM_MONITOR
  • NAMESPACE_ADMIN

Prerequisites

The following conditions must be met in order to call this operation.

  • Namespace and Bucket should exist

The bucket's usage for the requested time period.

sizeunit Unit to be used for calculating the size on disk (KB, MB, GB, KiB, MiB, GiB and BYTE. GB is default value). For legacy compatibility, KB, MB, and GB Units are considered equivalent to KiB, MiB, and GiB respectively. If needed, use BYTE Unit to convert to KB, MB and GB.

get.summary: Get Namespace Billing Info with Optional Bucket Detail specifying {NS}
get.description:
Get Namespace Billing Info

GET /object/billing/namespace/{namespace}/info

Gets billing details for the specified namespace and bucket details. Due to the fact that sampling all buckets in a namespace takes some time, it is possible that sum of the bucket values will not equal the corresponding namespace value. Optionally specifying Bucket Details parameter.

namespace Namespace to get information about

Query Parameters

include_bucket_detail Optional (default=false). If true, include information about all the buckets owned by this namespace. When bucket details are included, the total size on disk can be different to the total size without bucket details. This is due to bucket size being rounded and summed to give the total size.
marker Optional. Used to continue a truncated response. Omit this parameter on the first request.
sizeunit Unit to be used for calculating the size on disk (KB, MB, GB, KiB, MiB, GiB and BYTE. GB is default value). For legacy compatibility, KB, MB, and GB Units are considered equivalent to KiB, MiB, and GiB respectively. If needed, use BYTE Unit to convert to KB, MB and GB.

Required Roles

Requires one of the following roles:

  • SYSTEM_ADMIN
  • SYSTEM_MONITOR
  • NAMESPACE_ADMIN

Response Body

The response contains information about a namespace and its buckets.

GET /object/billing/namespace/{namespace}/sample

Gets billing details for the specified namespace, interval and, optionally, buckets. This method will return one and only one time sample. If the start_time and end_time do not align with a sampled time period, the matching samples will be aggregated. Optionally including Bucket Details with parameter.

Check the response for the actual time period sampled. Due to the fact that sampling all buckets in a namespace takes some time, it is possible that sum of the bucket values will not equal the corresponding namespace value.

namespace Namespace to get information about

Query Parameters

start_time Starting time (inclusive) for the sample(s) in ISO-8601 minute format. Must be a multiple of 5 minutes or an error will be returned.
end_time Ending time (exclusive) for the sample(s) in ISO-8601 minute format. Must be a multiple of 5 minutes or an error will be returned.
include_bucket_detail Optional (default=false). If true, include information about all the buckets owned by this namespace.
marker Optional. Used to continue a truncated response. Omit this parameter on the first request.
sizeunit Unit to be used for calculating the size on disk (KB, MB, GB, KiB, MiB, GiB and BYTE. GB is default value). For legacy compatibility, KB, MB, and GB Units are considered equivalent to KiB, MiB, and GiB respectively. If needed, use BYTE Unit to convert to KB, MB and GB.

Required Roles

Requires one of the following roles:

  • SYSTEM_ADMIN
  • SYSTEM_MONITOR
  • NAMESPACE_ADMIN

Response Body

The response contains the billing information for the given time period for the namespace and its buckets.

post.description:
Deactivate Bucket

POST /object/bucket/{bucketName}/deactivate

Deletes the specified bucket.

bucketName Bucket name to be deleted

Query Parameters

namespace Namespace associated. If it is null, then current user's namespace is used.
emptyBucket Optional: true | false (default). If emptyBucket=true the contents of the bucket will be emptied as part of the delete. The request will return a 202 Accepted if the bucket is not already empty and cleanup was initiated to run in the background. The bucket will be read only during the operation. If the task successfully removes all related items the bucket will be deleted. If the task is unable to remove all items or is aborted the bucket will be put back into a writable state. Progress can be monitored through call to: /object/bucket/{bucketName}/empty-bucket-status

If emptyBucket=false or not present the delete bucket operation will fail if the bucket is not empty.

Required Roles

Requires one of the following roles:

  • SYSTEM_ADMIN

Prerequisites

The following conditions must be met in order to call this operation.

  • Only authenticated users can delete buckets
  • Bucket name should not be null or empty

No Request Payload

Response Body

Response indicating success, failure, or accepted (when emptyBucket == true) of the bucket delete operation.

Update Bucket Is Stale Allowed

POST /object/bucket/{bucketName}/isstaleallowed

Updates isStaleAllowed details for the specified bucket in order to enable access to the bucket during a temporary site outage. If namespace does not exist in the request payload, the current user's namespace is used.

If you set this flag ON, and a temporary site outage occurs, objects that you access in this bucket might have been updated at the failed site but changes might not have been propagated to the site from which you are accessing the object. Hence, you are prepared to accept that the objects you read might not be up to date.

If the flag is turned OFF, data in the zone which has the temporary outage is not available for access from other zones and object reads for data which has its primary in the failed site will fail.

Validates bucket configuration during ADO updates by ensuring the read-only flag is enabled for FSA buckets and disabled for non-FSA buckets, throwing an error for any invalid setting.

bucketName Name of the bucket for which isStaleAllowed is to be updated

Required Roles

Bucket Policy on the specified bucket.

  • Version: Specifies the policy language version.
  • Id: Specifies an optional identifier for the policy.
  • Statement: Contains the policies.
  • Sid: It is an optional identifier that is provided for the policy statement.
  • Effect: Specifies whether the statement will result in an allow or an explicit deny.
  • Principal: Specifies the user.
  • NotPrincipal: Use the NotPrincipal element to specify an exception to a list of principals.
  • Action: Specifies the action or actions that will be allowed or denied. Statements must include either an Action or NotAction element.
  • NotAction: Specifies an advanced policy element that explicitly matches everything except a list of actions.
  • Resource: Specifies the object or objects that the statement covers.
  • NotResource: Specifies an advanced policy element that explicitly matches everything except a list of resources.
  • Condition: An optional element which specifies conditions for when a policy is in effect.

put.description:
Set Eventual Reads For Bucket

PUT /object/bucket/{bucketName}/set-local-object-metadata-reads

Sets local object metadata read flag to enable or disable OBS eventual reads for OBS CAS ADO RW buckets.

If the local object metadata reads are enabled then the OBS CAS ADO RW buckets will try to read the object metadata from the locally replicated data; this improves availability and improves latency if data was replicated and the object or bucket was created on remote VDC and the remote VDC is far away. But this may result in stale object metadata being returned if the object metadata is not fully replicated to the local VDC, the object can be still not deleted or returned Litigation Hold or Event Based Retention information can be outdated. If object metadata was not replicated then it will be requested from remote VDC.

Path Parameters

bucketName Bucket name for which the OBS CAS local object metadata reads should be updated.

Query Parameters

namespace Namespace of the bucket for which the OBS CAS local object metadata reads should be updated.
enabled Enable or disable OBS CAS local object metadata reads on the bucket buckets.

Required Roles

This call has no restrictions.

Prerequisites

The following conditions must be met in order to call this operation:

  • Bucket name should not be null or empty.
  • Bucket must exist.
  • Namespace name should not be null or empty.
  • Namespace must exist.

No request payload.

Response indicating Success or Failure of the operation.

DurationSeconds The temporary credentials are valid for the specified duration, from 900 seconds (15 minutes) up to a maximum of 129,600 seconds (36 hours). The default session duration is 43,200 seconds (12 hours).

PolicyArns.member.N The ARNs of the IAM managed policies that you want to use as managed session policies. The policies must exist in the same account as an IAM user.

put.description:
Put Compress Percentage Threshold

PUT /compression/compress-percentage-threshold/{value}

Update current compress percentage threshold. Compress percentage threshold means writing compressed data block only if it results in saving at least the given percentage of space. For example: 50 means writing compressed data block only if it results in saving at least 50% of space. If the original data block is 2M, and the compressed is 1.2M, it will not be compressed because it only saves 40%.

Path Parameters

Parameter Description
value Compress percentage threshold to be set.

Required Roles

Requires one of the following roles:

  • SYSTEM_ADMIN

Request Payload

No Request Payload

Response Body
